question:
Will Elon Musk post 320-339 tweets from October 31 to November 7, 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from October 31 12:00 PM ET to November 7, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

evidences.title: Elon Musk posts 154 times a day. Where does he find the ...
evidences.content: advertisement ❮❯ India Today’s OSINT team analysed Musk’s posting frequency over the past two years using Social Blade and found a sharp spike in posts leading up to the US elections in 2024. As the largest donor in the 2024 election cycle backing President Trump, Musk endorsed Trump’s candidacy in July 2024—a turning point that coincided with a dramatic rise in his posting frequency. Musk had posted 29,172 times on X in 2024, with 18,335 of those posts coming after his endorsement. His most intense posting period was between November 18 and November 25, when he averaged 154 posts per day. This was also the time when Musk outlined his plans for his new role as head of DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency). [...] Live TV Share Advertisement In May 2024, Musk averaged 332 posts per week, peaking at 977 posts per week in November. (AFP photo)") In May 2024, Elon Musk averaged 332 posts per week, peaking at 977 posts per week in November. evidences.title: [PDF] The Vibes are Off: Did Elon Musk Push Academics off Twitter?
evidences.content: August 2, 2024 Abstract Twitter has been a prominent forum for academics communicating online, both among themselves and with policymakers or the broader public. Elon Musk’s take-over of the company brought sweeping change to many aspects of the platform, including the public access to its data; Twitter’s approach to censorship and mis/disinformation; and tweaks to the affordances of the platform. In this letter, we take up a narrower empirical question: what did Elon Musk’s takeover of the platform mean for this academic ecosystem? Using a snowball sample of more than 15,700 academic accounts from the fields of economics, political science, sociology, and psychology, we show that academics in these fields reduced their “engagement” with the platform, measured with either the number of [...] 1Throughout this paper, we use the term “engagement” to refer to the principle behaviors associated with Twitter: posting original content, replying to others’ posts with original content, retweeting others’ posts (i.e., rebroadcasting without annotation), quote-tweeting others’ posts (i.e., rebroadcasting with annotation), and favoriting others’ posts. 3 Twitter has thus played a significan...

[...] To see whether Musk has done this again in the leadup to the US election, we compared Musk’s engagement metrics – such as the number of views, retweets and likes – with a set of other prominent political accounts on the social media platform. The data spans the period from January 1 2024 to October 25 2024. evidences.title: A Content Analysis of Donald Trump's and Elon Musk's Tweets
evidences.content: ## Results ### General Tweet Characteristics and Engagement Patterns #### Tweet Volume and Frequency Analysis of the 2,391 tweets reveals distinct posting behaviors between Donald Trump and Elon Musk. Trump demonstrated a higher frequency of Twitter/X/X communication, averaging 7.3 tweets per day during active periods, compared to Musk's 3.8 tweets per day (table 2). Trump's tweeting pattern showed pronounced spikes during political events and controversies, while Musk's posting frequency remained more consistent with occasional surges around product launches and company announcements. Table 2. Tweet Frequency and Volume Characteristics. [...] Engagement effectiveness patterns observed in the normalized analysis correspond with established research on audience relationship dynamics in leadership communication. Kumar et al. (2024) examined engagement patterns across 500 leaders with follower bases ranging from 100,000 to 50 million, identifying two distinct effectiveness profiles. “Intensity-focused” leaders achieved higher per-follower engagement rates through emotionally-charged messaging and direct audience appeals, while “consistency-focused” leaders maintained stable engagem...

evidences.title: How Traders Are Making Thousands Counting Elon Musk's ...
evidences.content: splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. You buy the bracket you think he lands in, and if you're right, your share pays out a dollar. If you're wrong, they go to zero. Right now, the current week, February 24th to March 3rd, the market is pricing 300 to 319 tweets as the most likely outcome at 24%. The bracket below it, 280 to 299 is 22% and 320 to 339 is also 22%. So the market thinks Elon is going to post somewhere around 300 posts this week, roughly 43 tweets a day, almost two tweets an hour, every waking hour for seven straight days. And this isn't a one-off. There are four overlapping Elon tweet markets running right now. The weekly, a rolling 7-day, a 2-day window, and a daily. Combined volume across all of them, over 52 million, $15 million traded today. Let that [...] There's a market on Poly Market right now with $23 million in volume, $15 million traded in the last 24 hours. And it's not about war. It's not about an election. It's not even about crypto. It's about how many times Elon Musk is tweeting this week. 30 different brackets, 0 to 19 tweets, 20 to 39 all the way up to 580 plus. Thousands of people spending real money trying to predict the posti...

evidences.title: Elon Musk definitely tweets too much
evidences.content: ## Methodology and Assumptions ( This analysis was made possible by a scraper I built that gathers tweets from Elon’s timeline. On Twitter, a person’s timeline displays thevast majorityof their tweets, but for unexplained reasons, depending on when you load someone’s Timeline, some tweets don’t show up. I ran my scraper twice, on September 7th and September 16th. I took all tweets that showed up on either scraping attempt. A small number of Tweets from the time period may have fallen through the cracks, but we can consider this aconservative limitation.The given numbers in my figures cannot be any _lower._ In fact, they might actually be _worse._ [...] # Elon Musk definitely tweets too much - Stats with Sasa Skip to content Image 1 Stats with Sasa Easy to digest analytics. Popular Posts Blog Current Events Fact or Fiction Sports The devastation of Gaza Richmond Socials Business Inquiries About Me . For most people, that would be sufficient to pull years of data. For example, I was able to pull over 2 years of data for Virginia’s Senate candidates Tim Kaine and Hung Cao despite this limitation. In comparison, I was only able to pull under 50 days of Elon Musk’s tweets.You read th...

evidences.title: Instagram
evidences.content: xAI, Elon Musk’s AI startup, is expected to close a $15 billion funding round in December at a $230 billion pre-money valuation. The raise — which Musk had previously disputed on X — underscores the surging investor appetite for foundational AI models, mirroring recent multibillion-dollar rounds by OpenAI and Anthropic. Sources said xAI plans to use much of the capital to acquire GPUs needed to power its large language models, including Grok, the company’s chatbot that has faced criticism for spreading hate speech. The round comes months after Musk merged xAI with X in a deal that valued the social media platform at $33 billion. FOUNDER: Elon Musk AMOUNT: $15,000,000,000 VALUATION: $230,000,000,000 HQ: #PaloAlto #California #VentureCapital #xAI #ElonMusk #TradedVC

evidences.title: Polymarket XTracker API and Elon Musk Tweet Count Markets
evidences.content: Counting rules across markets that reference the tracker: main feed posts, quote posts, and reposts count toward the total. Standard replies do not count unless they appear in the main feed view. Deleted posts count if the tracker captured them before removal, roughly a five minute window. The Post Counter is the primary source, with X itself as a backup if the tracker has a gap. Because the post list is exportable, users can audit the count.
